project_overview.project_name: Guinea Support to Local Governance Project 2 (PAGL II)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve national and local capacity for (i) efficient and accountable service delivery to communities, (ii) climate-resilient local development, and (iii) inclusive citizen engagement
project_overview.beneficiaries: Population of 356 targeted communes in Guinea; 296,000 elected officials, national and local government cadres, civil society representatives and communities expected to benefit from capacity-strengthening activities

primary_locations: Rural and urban communes outside Conakry
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - Guinea is characterized as fragile with high political and governance risks; conflicts driven by resource access, agro-pastoral tensions, mining-related disputes, and inter-community tensions; 64% of population lives in climate-vulnerable areas

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Supporting national and local capacity for efficient and accountable management of local development resources
key_components.description: Supports ANAFIC's expanded mandate to manage FNDL, FODEL, and FODECCON; strengthens LG capacity for resource mobilization and accountable management; supports regional training centers; includes performance-based conditions for FNDL/FODEL execution and public accountant deployment

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Improving national and local capacity for climate-resilient local development
key_components.description: Integrates climate resilience into local development planning through participatory climate risk diagnosis; develops monitoring and reporting tools; supports training on climate adaptation; includes performance-based conditions for climate-resilient investment prioritization

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Strengthening mechanisms for inclusive citizen engagement in local governance
key_components.description: Empowers women, youth, and vulnerable groups in local development; scales up Early Warning and Response System for conflict prevention; strengthens citizen engagement and accountability mechanisms; supports NGO/CBO partnerships; includes performance-based conditions for inclusion and citizen engagement

Supports PIU management embedded in ANAFIC; establishes Environmental and Social Management System; develops communication strategy; recruits gender specialist with GBV expertise and social inclusion specialist
Zero-budget emergency contingency fund that can be triggered for natural disasters or pandemics through formal declaration of national emergency

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Territorial Administration and Decentralization (MATD); National Agency for Local Government Financing (ANAFIC); Project Implementation Unit embedded within ANAFIC
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Steering Committee chaired by General Secretary of MATD with ANAFIC as Secretariat; ANAFIC Board of Directors; Regional branches (ARA-ANAFIC); Decentralized Technical Services (STD) at prefectural level; Local committees (COPIL, COSEP, CGAR) at commune level; Local Development Agents (ADLs) and Community Facilitators (ACs) at district level
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers; Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD); use of STEP system; prior review for contracts above thresholds; procurement specialist and assistant at national level; one procurement analyst per region
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: ANAFIC updates M&E framework with gender and age-disaggregated data; M&E team at national level coordinated with regional M&E specialists; linked to national M&E system for decentralization policy; independent verification agent hired to verify PBC achievement; quarterly unaudited interim financial reports; annual external audits

identified_risks.risk_description: Guinea characterized by fragile political and governance environment; September 2021 military coup; exposure to external shocks; underemployed youth; lack of popular support for state institutions; political instrumentalization of ethnicity; limited transparency and accountability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Transitional government assurances of continued support to programming; commitment to decentralization reform reflected in 2022-2025 PRI; large share of budget conditioned on achievement of key intermediary results via PBC mechanism

identified_risks.risk_description: Vulnerability to external shocks (Ebola, COVID-19, commodity price declines); risks of emerging or continuing external/domestic imbalances; mining revenue volatility
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Country's continued engagement in macroeconomic stabilization programs; project design leverages existing FNDL/FODEL mechanisms rather than adding new funds

identified_risks.risk_description: ANAFIC's expanded mandate to manage FODEL and FODECCON presents absorption and technical capacity challenges; urban development complexity outside current ANAFIC expertise; recent government restructuring with 14,000 civil servants retired and replaced requiring training
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project will support extensive capacity assessment of ANAFIC to identify structural, technical, and staffing adjustments needed; road map for absorption of FODEL functions; project focuses on FNDL and FODEL where capacity exists; does not extend support to FODECCON

identified_risks.risk_description: Political volatility raises corruption and fiduciary risks; LG accounts frozen for extended period due to verification process; concerns over management and delivery of local development funds
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Solid FM and fiduciary procedures; digitization of LG financial reports; reinforcement of ANAFIC and LG capacity for transparent management; incentivizing timely FNDL/FODEL transfers and execution; PBC incentives around ADL/AC network maintenance

identified_risks.risk_description: Broad range of stakeholders involved in implementation; coordination challenges across multiple levels and agencies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project emphasizes role of LG and sub-commune-level positions and community committees; structures have proved sustainable under PACV and PAGL; deeply embedded in local development processes

identified_risks.risk_description: Conflicts driven by resource access, agro-pastoral tensions, mining-related disputes; inter-community tensions; climate change exacerbating resource competition
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Component 3 supports Early Warning and Response System scaled to all at-risk communes; conflict prevention and mediation mechanisms; citizen engagement and accountability mechanisms to build trust; climate resilience integration to reduce resource-driven tensions

triggered_safeguard_policies: Environmental and Social Standard 1 (ESS1) - Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
Environmental and Social Standard 2 (ESS2) - Labor and Working Conditions
Environmental and Social Standard 3 (ESS3) - Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (not currently relevant)
Environmental and Social Standard 4 (ESS4) - Community Health and Safety
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM established and operational; updated from PAGL; special provisions for receiving and managing sensitive complaints related to sexual exploitation and abuse/sexual harassment; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; World Bank Inspection Panel available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender specialist with expertise in gender-based violence to be recruited; women face barriers to participation in local civil life; only 15% of LG representatives are women; project targets increased representation of women in leadership roles (chair, treasurer, women's representative) in local committees; training on gender inclusion and transformative grassroots leadership; support for women's representation in ADL positions; gender-disaggregated indicators; coordination with SWEDD 2 Project on harmful social norms
vulnerable_groups: Women (particularly rural women and those in poorest quintiles)
Farmers and herder communities in conflict-prone mining zones

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly addresses conflict risks through multiple mechanisms: Early Warning and Response System (EWRS) scaled to all at-risk communes for prevention and mediation of local conflicts including those related to climate change impacts; conflict management mechanisms including community-based GRMs; participatory budgeting and citizen engagement to build trust and reduce tensions; integration of conflict-sensitive approaches in local development planning; support for dispute mediation mechanisms; selection of communes for EWRS scale-up informed by study considering local-level climate and conflict-related risks
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Guinea classified as highly fragile with 64% of population in climate-vulnerable areas; conflicts driven by access to and management of resources, sociopolitical and inter-community tensions, access to basic services; agro-pastoral conflicts over natural resources; mining-related disputes; land and property conflicts; climate change pressures provoking clashes between farmers, herders, and fishermen; weak service delivery undermining state legitimacy; poor governance and accountability; limited channels for citizen engagement; marginalized vulnerable groups; political transition and government restructuring creating institutional gaps
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project addresses security through: citizen engagement mechanisms to build trust in institutions; accountability mechanisms to reduce grievances; conflict prevention and early warning systems; climate resilience to reduce resource-driven tensions; inclusion of vulnerable groups to address marginalization; support for local dispute resolution mechanisms; coordination with national security and governance priorities; emphasis on transparent and accountable local governance
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no - Project does not involve land acquisition, economic displacement, or restriction of access to natural resources; no civil works requiring resettlement; however, project recognizes climate change may increase migration pressures from rural to urban areas due to agricultural stress
